title: 2 Ariana Grande concerts at TD Garden are rescheduled
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Ariana Grande's TD Garden concerts on July 22 and 24 are rescheduled to July 23 and 26.
The rescheduling is due to production challenges ensuring the full show is delivered safely.
Tickets for the original dates will be honored, and ticket holders will receive additional information.

title: Bruins prospect Dean Letourneau has gone from a project to a legitimate blue-chip talent
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Dean Letourneau scored 22 goals in his sophomore year at Boston College, rebounding from a disappointing freshman season.
The 6-foot-7 prospect was drafted 25th overall by the Bruins in 2024, showing significant improvement in Hockey East play.
Letourneau plans to return to Boston College for another year, aiming to further develop before attempting a pro hockey leap.

title: Why did City Hall’s FIFA Fan Festival close with some World Cup games left to play?
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston's FIFA Fan Festival at City Hall closed after the World Cup group stage, despite matches continuing.
More than 155,000 people from 108 countries attended the 16-day festival at City Hall Plaza.
Mayor Michelle Wu cited financial constraints for the festival's early closure, despite hopes to extend it.

title: Could Bruins be a viable landing spot for Darnell Nurse via trade?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Darnell Nurse has requested a trade from the Edmonton Oilers after 12 seasons with the team.
The Boston Bruins are interested in adding a top-four defenseman, with Nurse being a potential option despite his $9.25 million cap hit.
The Bruins must navigate salary cap issues to acquire Nurse, as they currently have $7.7 million in cap space available.

title: Car carrying family of 3 pulled from Seekonk River in Rhode Island
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A car with a family of three went into the Seekonk River, Rhode Island, near the Taft Street boat ramp Sunday evening.
Pawtucket police and state agencies worked to recover the car Monday afternoon; occupants are believed to be still inside.
Recovery efforts face challenges due to strong currents and poor visibility; identities and conditions of occupants remain unknown.

title: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes restaurant, shifts focus back to food truck
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Jamaica Mi Hungry has closed its Downtown Boston restaurant, ending its brick-and-mortar locations after nearly seven years.
The business will focus on catering and a food truck with stops at Kendall Square, MIT, and Boston Common.
Chef Ernie Campbell founded Jamaica Mi Hungry in 2012, with the brand receiving multiple awards for its food.

title: Report finds former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used ‘horrific’ language in texts
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Canton officials released a report detailing offensive texts by former Sgt. Sean Goode, including slurs against various ethnic and minority groups.
The Massachusetts Peace Officer Standards and Training Commission suspended Goode's certification, barring him from law enforcement throughout the state.
Goode resigned amid an internal affairs investigation, which began after his messages surfaced in a lawsuit by Karen Read.

title: Alvin Campbell Jr., serial rapist and AG’s brother, receives life in prison
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr., convicted of raping five women and attempting another assault, sentenced to life in prison.
Campbell posed as an Uber driver to target victims outside Boston bars from 2017 to 2019.
He is the brother of Attorney General Andrea Campbell and faces additional charges from separate incidents.

title: Officials identify man killed in wrong-way crash in Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Riley Sergi, 28, from Haverhill, was killed in a wrong-way crash on I-93 in Boston on Sunday.
Antone Carvalho, 81, of Somerset, drove the wrong way and will face court in connection with the crash.
A family of four was hospitalized after the collision, with no update on their condition as of Monday.

title: 5 takeaways from Paraguay’s upset win over Germany in World Cup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paraguay defeated Germany 4-3 in penalty kicks at 'Boston Stadium' to advance to the World Cup Round of 16.
Germany's Jonathan Tah's extra-time goal was disallowed by VAR due to a foul, keeping the score tied at 1-1.
Paraguay's José Canale scored the winning penalty after Jonathan Tah missed his attempt for Germany.
